page: Microsoft Azure E16-4s_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the Esv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz, with 2 physical cores and 2 threads per core. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32.0 GB per core. The instance has no bundled local storage, though it is Premium Storage capable, and contains no GPUs.

Performance benchmarks show strong single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2126.0 and strong single-threaded gzip compression at 31.33 GB/sec. However, multi-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 3989.54 and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 26.03 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth is also in the lower tier, with uncached large block read speeds at 34.79 GB/sec and write speeds at 24.77 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET operations also fall into the weaker performance category, with Redis achieving 5.68 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E16-4s_v6 offers a high density of memory relative to its compute resources, making it a specialized option for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel processing power. The inclusion of advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions assists with specific vector and matrix math operations. This server is best suited for memory-intensive applications with low CPU scaling requirements, such as medium-sized in-memory caches, lightweight database hosting, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure E16-4s_v6 is a memory-optimized x86_64 server instance from the Esv6 family, powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs and a substantial 128.0 GB of RAM, providing 32.0 GB of memory per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Benchmark data reveals strong single-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2126.0 and high single-threaded gzip compression speeds. Conversely, multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks are relatively weak, with uncached large-block read speeds at 34.79 GB/sec. Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, this instance is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive workloads that do not demand high parallel compute scaling. It is highly suited for in-memory caching, lightweight database operations, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16-4s_v6 is a memory-optimized x86_64 server featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM. It is designed for memory-intensive workloads requiring strong single-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16-4s_v6 is a memory-optimized server with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and strong single-core performance.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring a high memory-to-core ratio and strong single-threaded performance for memory-bound enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs running at 4.2 GHz
128.0 GB of RAM providing an exceptional 32.0 GB per core ratio
Strong single-core performance with high single-threaded compression speeds
No bundled local storage, configured for Premium Storage integration
Best suited for memory-bound applications and in-memory databases
